item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
God, this is a difficult vote to make. The imagery here is impressively evocative, and I absolutely adore it. The overall atmosphere created by the description here is great.

However, the ending, what it all boils down to, is ultimately disappointing. It's just the same 'super powerful creature is contained, but not for much longer' that we've seen many times before, and the imagery didn't quite outweigh this problem for me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is incredibly eerie, even by modern site standards. Even if the story or its setup may not ultimately suffice (although I do like the description of the doctor's attempts to both save his daughter(s) and prevent anything terrible from occurring), the tense atmosphere does it justice.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
After some thought, i decided to downvote to see a greater chance of this being reworked in the future.

The setting given is good, though some details are unnecessary, but the piece ultimately does nothing with it.

For one, the location is enormous for no reason. Since it is a pocket reallity, it being infinite isn't a huge thing unless some exploration logs or observations actually do something with this infinity. The access is over-complicated, again for no good reason, nothing in the piece makes it specially hard to contain. It is subtler, but still the same as a "Titanium-Concrete Cage".

After a lot of reading just to get over an overly-complex entrance and wasted infinite space, we get the picture of the room, the true core of the article… and that is that. Nothing in the piece explains or even hint to whatever is happening here or why anyone should care that much. Instead, extraneous pieces are brought just to force everything to piece together. Suddenly we are informed that Dr. Hulk created this and left some notes, and we believe those notes because why not?

On my final issue, there is no reason or payoff on it being Keter. No matter how dangerous it is on the account of their daddy, it clearly falls into Euclid category. To say that "it must be Keter 'cause it is that important" is to undermine all other SCPs under Foundation care.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I feel like Dr. Banning's notes need to be in this. It feels like a lot of writers look at 087 and go "oh, yeah, I'll avoid including the thing that explains the article, and that'll be the payoff", but it works with 087 because the third log works pretty well as a payoff, with the Spooky Ghost in it finally doing something, and the fourth log would likely overexplain the article.

With this there isn't a payoff. There's a setup, and an interesting one, but it feels more like the premise for something than the "something" itself.

item.title: Junior Staff Post - Crosslink Proposal
item.content:encoded:
Timer expired - edit implemented.

The WikiWalk team is proposing edits to this page. All proposed edits will be in a collapsible below this post, and after 7 days if there are no serious objections to the edits, the feedback received will be taken into account as the edits are made. If you have questions not directly related to the article about this project, please PM a member of the WikiWalk Team.

+ Proposed Edits
- Proposed Edits

The crosslink proposed is to link SCP-2464 to the Site-81 dossier. The article in question refers to the site as part of its containment procedures. In addition, the author of the article is also the author of the site dossier.

Editing this:

Individuals who gain access past Area-2464-1 are to be contained, brought to Site 81 for examination, and subsequently administered Class D amnestics or terminated.

To this:

Individuals who gain access past Area-2464-1 are to be contained, brought to Site 81 for examination, and subsequently administered Class D amnestics or terminated.

item.title: Re: A Tale of Two Sisters
item.content:encoded:
Firstly, good job, now small critiques.

I feel like the designations separating the checkpoints, the house, the sisters, and the area, in general, are a bit…. confusing. A redesignation to make the subject described clearer would be beneficial in my opinion. Thank you.

item.title: Re: why have no attempts to neutralize this SCP occured?
item.content:encoded: Because the Foundation contains things. This is how they do.

item.title: why have no attempts to neutralize this SCP occured?
item.content:encoded:
It seems like we're sustaining a tenuous situation for no good reason here. The mortality of the twins has not been established. Has anyone tried cutting 02? If it bleeds, it can be killed right? Why wait for 02 to break out of containment to use the nuke? why not place a nuke in the farmhouse and blow er up? What benefit does keeping them alive have?!

+1
Another fantastic kaktus-piece.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I love this to death, not just because of the surreal imagery, but mostly because it self show that a reality bender is NOT a good thing to be. Reality bending isn't a cool superpower, it's a disorder that can ruin your life, if not cause a stain in the fabric of spacetime.

These girls could have been so much. They could have graduated high school, gotten their dream careers, gotten married and had kids… Yet because of this disease, they can't do that. And they can't die, either. As long as -4 is maintained, they'll always be stuck like this, forever trapped in a limbo, unable to live or die. And the worst part is, it needs to be this way. It's either them, or potentially the entire universe.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well duh. Foundation information control can make a mine 'disappear' easy.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Can't find a Morris Limestone Mine in Indiana.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This has a really good concept and imagery.

I've critiqued this more at length in chat, but it does have issues: it's overly long and clunky and much of it should be cleaned up or cut. (Much like many of my own posts. :( )

Also, consider doing a little more with the reality bender. What you have here is good, but I wish there were more intriguing details.

+1'd though, because I still like it :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Having both area 2464-1 through area 2464-5 and SCP 2464-1 through SCP 2464-4 make the start of the article a bit confusing. Maybe change on of the sub-designations from numerals to letters?

Restricted access to SCP-2464-1 is to be maintained via 5 checkpoint stations (designated Area 2464-1 through -5) spaced roughly 5 km apart from each other throughout the primary access tunnel.

Wouldn't that make the tunnel at least 20km long? Seems way too long for a mining tunnel.

current models predict SCP-2464 will be unable to

Don't you mean "SCP-2464-4"?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really want to read the notes that Dr. Banning left.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Creepy, unsettling, just really nice. And thanks Scorpion451, I hadn't even thought of that! It would make sense that if they were twins they might both be reality-benders. My first read was that he had only had one daughter, and he had either grown the twin for her DNA/organ matches, or that his daughter had created it as a playmate but not given it sentience.

My only comment would be that I hope we can see some of the doctor's notes! Even if they're heavily redacted, it feels odd to hear that they exist and not be able to see them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I read this with my breath held in my throat. Such a sense of tension! Super good. The only problem is the huge buildup to the final collapsible, what could it be hiding?!, and then it just says keter class was approved. A thing we already know. I would chop that right off.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Oh fuck I remember this one. I love shit like this. More, more!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Now this is an keter. To me you nailed the detail level and the pacing of the reveal. Am I right in thinking that the -3 sister is some sort of anti-reality warper, or a reality warper herself? Regardless, that last evening they spent in normal spacetime had to have been one heck of a rough day for the fabric of reality.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Oop, good catch. I feex.

Also, yeah. That colored blob is a guy bending over, and the stone slab he's standing on in the entrance to -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think you've done a good job here, making this more tight and to the point. I think the removals haven't damaged the spirit at all, and it's still very interesting and scary.

I was a little worried by the last addendum hinting at escalation, but I think you handled it subtly enough to make it work.

One thing that occurred to me on re-reading is that if -3 is unconscious (the majority of the time), her head would hang down unless supported in some way. Might be worth including 'neck' in the list of shackles. Not a huge thing.

Also, you've left a close collapsible in that doesn't have a corresponding opener. I didn't fix it myself because I didn't know how much you wanted to be collapsed - but you should fix this ASAP because it looks pretty bad!

And regarding the picture… even though you've said it's a limestone mine, I still can't figure it out! What's the scale? Is the coloured blob a person?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Alright, I went through and made some pretty significant edits based on your observations, and I can honestly say I like it a lot better now. The last note is gone, the addendum have been rearranged, and a few things have been cut or added.

As for the picture, it's actually an interior shot of a cutting wall from a limestone mine, given to me by a patient at our office. The reason it looks the way it does is because 1.) it's pretty well lit, and 2.) it's not a great picture. That being said, it is literally a picture of what I depict as the entrance to -1, so I figured I probably couldn't get better than that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The Hulk is Dr. Bruce Banner - but it's close enough that I also thought of the Hulk when I read it. A little bit immersion-breaking.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This one is okay, but really just okay in my opinion. I think the problem is that there's altogether too much fluff. The infinite space under the cavern is pretty well pointless; its connection to -2, -3, and -4 is tenuous and underdeveloped, which makes it look like filler.

You have, however, managed a really interesting mental image. Strange device with copper pipes feeding into a young girl. Viscous black fluid pouring from her mouth into her sister's mouth. Identical twins (which is really just a short-cut to creepy town), a poorly repaired farm house, constant dusk. It's reasonably surreal, I'll give it that. Some kind of half-remembered dream or something.

But there are flaws. It doesn't cash the emotional check, for one (an angle it's pretty clear you were trying hard for through the last addendum). I'm a dad, even, and I find it hard to relate for some reason. Maybe it's because you wrote the letter from the POV of a Foundation crony and not the POV of Dr. Banning. Maybe it's because "Dr. Banning" is also the name of the Incredible Hulk. Maybe I'm being a fudd. Nonetheless, it doesn't strike me.

Second major flaw I see is the restricted access. Perhaps I'm jaded or too used to looking at the Foundation from a Level 4+ perspective, but I don't see the need to keep that information secret. It seems to me that it would stress the importance of the classification and the research into repairing SCP-2464-4. Maybe it would make the assignment a bit more stressful, but considering the other kinds of Keter threats the Foundation deals with I don't think that would be a concern.

Despite my misgivings, the prose is solid, the narrative is clear, and most of the choices seem self-consistent and justified. You'll get a 0 from me, but it's an optimistic one.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Firstly: +1. This is very good.

However, it does have a few issues, which I will highlight on an individual basis.

Show line-by-line
Close

The image - I cannot for the life of me figure out how it relates to the SCP. It appears to be an underground, concrete-walled building like a garage. If it's meant to be the entrance in the limestone mine, it looks nothing like that.

as one travels further and further away from SCP-2464-2

Is this meant to be -1? It could easily be either, but I wanted to check.

SCP-2464-4 is a machine designed and constructed by the late Dr. Michael Banning

As this is your first mention of Banning, I think you should add a brief description of who he is - either here, or earlier, perhaps when you first mention the house (it's his house, right?). I was very confused when I read this - is he a Foundation doctor? He is mentioned like someone I should already know.

The apparent purpose of SCP-2464-4 is as a means of extending the lifespan of SCP-2464-3 by means of preventing SCP-2464-3 from aging

This sentence feels a bit redundant - the repetition of 'means of' highlighting this. You should consider shortening it.

entirely because of the difficulty in finding and containing these entities. It is entirely possible that,

Might want to consider a re-write to address the repetition of 'entirely'.

Finally, regarding the final addendum: I don't like it. It tells us nothing that the rest of the article doesn't tell us, and it just feels like a note directly from the author (not helped by the name being an obvious reference to your username) telling us that we should have feels about this. Feels would already be there - especially if you included, as I suggested earlier, a quick note indicating that the farmhouse is believed to be the property of Dr Banning, the father of -2 and -3.

But yeah I still really like it and my upvote stands even as-is.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hot damn, that is one creepy concept.
I like the buildup for this article, and the pay off is also pretty neat.
Well done. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It would have worked even without bullets nor bolding.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Very well done. The entire setup is horrifying on its own, but the reason for it is even more so.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For organizational purposes. I thought bullets looked kind of awkward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why are the sub-SCPs bolded?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Always nice to see a reality bender handled well, especially with the mental image of that machine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Good job, djkaktus. Very surreal mental picture, and the concept is pretty damn interesting.

item.title: A Tale of Two Sisters
item.content:encoded:
I wanted to keep this scarce for any additional detail, because I feel like detail makes it bland. If you have questions, that's good.

Once again, my thanks to those who looked at this in chat and gave comments/critiques, notably Jekeled and Devereaux. It is always appreciated! :D

Edit: The image in this article was originally taken by an acquaintance of mine in 2014, who released it to me with express permission to release it here under Creative Commons.
